page: Microsoft Azure FX48-24mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at a high base clock speed of 4.2 GHz. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization, the instance provides 12 physical cores and 24 vCPUs. The hardware configuration is heavily memory-optimized, featuring 1008.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 42.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 2835 GB, and there are no GPU accelerators present on this system.

Benchmark data reveals top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core and text-processing workloads, placing this server in the top 10% of tested systems due to its high clock frequency. Large-block memory bandwidth for both read and write operations is strong, ranking in the top 25%, as do single-threaded compression and decompression speeds. Multi-core performance metrics, including stress-ng and Passmark CPU scores, fall into the average tier, reflecting the moderate physical core count relative to the massive memory footprint.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers high memory density per vCPU. This configuration is advantageous for memory-bound applications that do not scale linearly with high core counts, allowing organizations to avoid the licensing and infrastructure overhead of over-provisioning compute resources. The primary tradeoff is the moderate parallel processing capacity, meaning workloads requiring massive multi-core scaling may find better efficiency elsewhere.

This server is best suited for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large in-memory databases, caching layers, and heavy text-processing tasks that benefit from high single-core frequency and a substantial RAM capacity.
description: The Microsoft Azure FX48-24mds_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring a 12-core Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, delivering 24 vCPUs via Hyper-V. It is equipped with 1008.0 GB of RAM and 2835 GB of local storage, with no GPU accelerators. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core and text-processing performance, alongside strong large-block memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression speeds. Conversely, multi-core and database operations sit in the average tier due to the moderate core count. This resource distribution makes the instance highly cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that require high single-thread performance but do not scale across massive parallel core arrays. It is best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.

tagline: A high-frequency, memory-dense server featuring over one terabyte of RAM and fast single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
